query: German style creamed spinach side dish with onion, garlic, cream, and nutmeg

draft.title: Rahmspinat
draft.description: German style creamed spinach side dish with onion, garlic, cream, and nutmeg, perfect for 4 servings.

steps.text: If using fresh spinach, wash thoroughly and remove thick stems. Blanch in boiling salted water for 1-2 minutes, then drain and squeeze out excess water. If frozen, thaw and drain.
Melt butter in a large pan over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the prepared spinach to the pan and stir well to combine with onion and garlic.
Pour in the cream and stir. Let it simmer gently for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cream thickens slightly.
Season with salt, freshly ground black pepper, and freshly grated nutmeg to taste. Stir well and remove from heat.
Serve warm as a traditional German side dish.
